title: Vellozia retinervis
intro: Vellozia retinervis, also known as the "Brazilian Vellozia," is a plant species belonging to the Velloziaceae family, native to Brazil. The health benefits of Vellozia retinervis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Vellozia retinervis is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Vellozia retinervis is a species of flowering plant in the family Velloziaceae. This plant is native to Brazil and is a member of the genus Vellozia, which comprises about 100 species of plants. The Velloziaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as pipcress or spike-rush, and they are found in tropical and subtropical regions. Vellozia retinervis is classified as a monocot, which means it has one cotyledon in its seedling stage. The classification of Vellozia retinervis is also further categorized as a member of the order Pandanales.
